senses.definition: a strength sport consisting of three lifts: the squat, bench press, and deadlift
a strength sport for athletes with physical disabilities, lifting maximum weights in three lifts

examples.x: She has been training hard for the para powerlifting competition next month.
Para powerlifting has gained popularity as a competitive sport for athletes with physical disabilities.
His dedication to para powerlifting has inspired many others with disabilities to take up the sport.
The para powerlifting event at the Paralympic Games showcases incredible strength and determination.
The gym has specialized equipment for para powerlifting training sessions.

senses.definition: (weightlifting) an Olympic lift where the lifter lifts the barbell from the floor to overhead in one continuous motion

examples.x: The snatch requires speed, strength, and precision.
She set a new personal record in the snatch competition.
Coaches emphasize proper form and technique in the snatch.
His snatch was smooth and powerful, earning him applause.
The snatch is a challenging but rewarding lift in weightlifting competitions.

senses.definition: a grip technique in weightlifting where the thumb is wrapped around the barbell, with the fingers and palm covering the thumb

examples.x: He improved his deadlift by using the hook grip technique.
The weightlifter found the hook grip provided better control over the barbell.
Beginners often struggle to get comfortable with the hook grip.
She opted for the hook grip to prevent the barbell from slipping during heavy lifts.
The weightlifter's hook grip allowed them to lift heavier weights with confidence.

senses.definition: the position in weightlifting where the barbell rests across the front of the shoulders and collarbone with the elbows lifted

examples.x: He struggled to maintain proper form in the front rack during the clean.
Beginners often find it challenging to achieve a comfortable front rack.
The coach emphasized the importance of a tight front rack for efficient jerks.
She worked on her mobility to improve her front rack flexibility.
The weightlifter's front rack was rock-solid as he prepared for the clean and jerk.

senses.definition: (weightlifting) the act of holding the barbell or weights directly above the head with fully extended arms

examples.x: She struggled to maintain the overhead position during her snatch lift.
He smoothly transitioned into the overhead position after completing his clean.
The athlete held the barbell steady in the overhead position for a few seconds before dropping it.
Achieving a stable overhead position is crucial for a successful jerk lift.
The coach emphasized the importance of wrist stability in the overhead position.

senses.definition: the maximum amount of weight an individual can lift for a single repetition of a given exercise

examples.x: He tested his bench press one-rep max to gauge his strength progress.
She achieved a new personal record by hitting her squat one-rep max.
Knowing your one-rep max helps tailor your workout program for optimal gains.
He attempted his deadlift one-rep max and successfully lifted the weight.
She focused on improving her one-rep max for the snatch ahead of the upcoming meet.

senses.definition: the phase in weightlifting where the lifter lifts the barbell off the ground, typically transitioning into a higher position such as the clean or snatch
an Olympic lift where the lifter lifts a barbell from the floor to the shoulders, clean, and then lifts it overhead, jerk, in two distinct movements

examples.x: He excelled in the clean and jerk, showcasing his strength and technique.
The clean and jerk is a complex lift that requires skill and power.
She set a new personal best in the clean and jerk competition.
His clean and jerk lift was impressive, earning him a spot on the podium.
They practiced clean and jerk drills to refine their lifting mechanics.

senses.definition: a type of weightlifting using kettlebells, typically involving swinging or lifting movements
the global governing body for the sport of weightlifting

examples.x: The International Weightlifting Federation sets the rules and regulations for Olympic weightlifting competitions.
The International Weightlifting Federation organizes world championships annually.
Doping violations are strictly monitored by the International Weightlifting Federation.
Changes to competition categories are announced by the International Weightlifting Federation.
Athletes must adhere to the guidelines established by the International Weightlifting Federation.

senses.definition: the global governing body for the sport of powerlifting

examples.x: The International Powerlifting Federation updates its rulebook periodically.
Lifters must follow the International Powerlifting Federation's guidelines to ensure fair play.
Records set at International Powerlifting Federation events are recognized worldwide.
National powerlifting bodies often affiliate with the International Powerlifting Federation.
The International Powerlifting Federation emphasizes safety and proper technique in the sport.

senses.definition: to lift or push a weight away from the body using the upper body muscles
a movement where the lifter lifts a barbell from shoulder height to overhead in one continuous motion, typically done from a standing position

examples.x: The press requires strong shoulders and core stability.
She added the press to her workout routine for upper body strength.
Coaches emphasize proper form and control in the press.
His strict press was steady and controlled, showcasing his technique.
The athlete's press technique improved with consistent practice.
